title: Explain the Fork/Join framework in Java. When should it be used?

answers.junior: Fork/Join framework helps split tasks into smaller parts and run them in parallel.
answers.middle: Fork/Join is for parallelism. It splits tasks into subtasks, processes them concurrently, and combines results. Use for CPU-intensive tasks.
answers.senior: Fork/Join uses work-stealing for efficient parallelism. Use RecursiveTask and RecursiveAction for splitting tasks. Prefer for divide-and-conquer algorithms.
answers.tricky: Fork/Join is ideal for recursive, parallel computation. Analyze task granularity and thread pool sizing. Monitor for contention and overhead. Prefer for large, CPU-bound workloads.
